_comment: Shared, character-independent expression vocabulary. Keys are the emoji allow-list exposed by set_expression(). Each entry maps an emoji to prompt fragments appended to a preset's base positive/negative prompts, plus an optional 'denoise' override. A preset may override any field via its own 'expression_overrides'. CLIP cannot read emoji, so this mapping to natural language is what actually reaches the sampler.
_denoise_note: Denoise is tuned to how much the reference image's geometry has to change to render the expression, not to how emotionally 'intense' it is. Closed-mouth, subtle-muscle expressions (neutral, content, smirk) get the lowest denoise so identity stays locked. Anything that opens the mouth or widens the eyes a lot (grin, laughing, surprised, shocked) needs a higher denoise budget to actually render, even if the emotion itself isn't extreme.

😊.label: smile
😊.positive: warm friendly smile, soft happy eyes

😃.label: grin
😃.positive: big cheerful grin, bright wide eyes

😂.label: laughing
😂.positive: laughing joyfully, eyes crinkled shut, open smile

😌.label: content
😌.positive: content peaceful expression, gentle closed-eye smile

😉.label: wink
😉.positive: playful wink, one eye closed, slight smirk

😏.label: smirk
😏.positive: sly confident smirk, one eyebrow raised

😢.label: sad
😢.positive: sad sorrowful expression, teary downturned eyes

😠.label: angry
😠.positive: angry expression, furrowed brow, hard glaring eyes

😮.label: surprised
😮.positive: surprised expression, wide eyes, slightly open mouth

😲.label: shocked
😲.positive: shocked expression, very wide eyes, mouth agape

😨.label: fearful
😨.positive: fearful anxious expression, worried raised brows

🤔.label: thinking
🤔.positive: thoughtful pensive expression, slightly furrowed brow

😴.label: sleepy
😴.positive: sleepy expression, half-closed heavy eyes, relaxed
